family_rules.reason: Schema and contract failures are deterministic by nature and must not be normalized away.
family_rules.retry_policy: no_retry_for_product_failures; one infrastructure rerun only if the original suite never reached test execution

family_rules.reason: Deterministic suites are the anti-flake foundation and lose value if rerun until green.
family_rules.retry_policy: no_retry unless the runner crashed before fixture execution began

family_rules.reason: Illegal-transition posture must stay reproducible from the same seed and cannot depend on ad hoc reruns.
family_rules.retry_policy: seed-preserving rerun only when infrastructure noise interrupted execution

family_rules.reason: API suites may experience infrastructure noise, but a blocking green result still must stay scope-identical and unquarantined.
family_rules.retry_policy: same-candidate same-scope rerun permitted for confirmed environment noise

family_rules.reason: Browser suites are slower and noisier than deterministic packs, but release green posture still cannot depend on quarantine or silent retries.
family_rules.retry_policy: same-candidate same-route-profile rerun allowed for actionability-safe infra noise only; preserve failing traces and screenshots

family_rules.reason: Native suites may need occasional environmental reruns, but a blocking pass still must be admissible and current.
family_rules.retry_policy: same-candidate same-scene rerun allowed only when device or simulator instability is isolated

family_rules.reason: Authority breadth evidence loses promotion value if reruns drift across profiles, operation families, or namespace isolation.
family_rules.retry_policy: rerun only with the identical enabled provider-profile set, identical namespace scope, and explicit preservation of exercised operation families

family_rules.reason: Restore or replay evidence is only meaningful if the claimed checkpoint and basis stay identical throughout rerun and admissibility.
family_rules.retry_policy: same-candidate same-checkpoint rerun only when the drill environment failed before evidence completion; replay cases otherwise stay single-shot and deterministic

family_rules.reason: Critical security or artifact integrity failures are not waiveable for blocking green posture.
family_rules.retry_policy: same-candidate rerun allowed only for infrastructure failures in the harness; security findings themselves are never retried to green

family_rules.reason: Canary and failure-mode evidence can be noisy, but promotion still requires a current, admissible, unwaived result.
family_rules.retry_policy: bounded same-candidate rerun allowed for environment noise, but SLO or error-budget failures remain product-significant until disproven with equivalent scope

global_rules.policy: A blocking suite result counts only when bound to the exact candidate, current, same-scope, and serialized with quarantine posture NONE and manual waiver posture NONE.
Any rerun used to recover from environment noise must preserve candidate identity, scope, and supporting compatibility or sandbox breadth boundaries.
Schema compatibility, authority mutation safety, restore or DR, signed build integrity, and critical security suites cannot be manually overridden into green release posture.
Flake triage keeps traces, screenshots, seeds, error objects, and correlation keys so diagnosability improves instead of being erased by reruns.

typed_gaps_or_deferred_decisions.summary: The governance process for reclassifying a family from blocking to non-blocking belongs to later platform or release operations work; ADR-008 only fixes the release-time constraint that the change cannot happen ad hoc during the judged release.
